summary: Verified live against codex-cli 0.133.0 (installed at /opt/homebrew/bin/codex, authenticated via ChatGPT login) plus developers.openai.com/codex docs. Codex CLI has a mature orchestration surface: `codex exec` for headless runs with a clean JSONL event stream (thread.started / turn.started / item.started / item.completed / turn.completed with usage), `-o/--output-last-message` and `--output-schema` for structured results, `codex exec resume <SESSION_ID>` for stateful follow-ups (verified context carry-over across processes), `-m` + `model_reasoning_effort` (minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh) for model/effort control, `--sandbox` + `--ask-for-approval never` (or `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`) for unattended operation, `codex app-server` (WebSocket/unix/stdio JSON-RPC, with TypeScript binding generation) and `codex mcp-server` for embedding, and scriptable `codex cloud exec/status/list/apply` for remote tasks. Exit code 0 on success, 1 on turn failure (verified). Local default config: model "gpt-5.5", effort "xhigh"; models cache lists gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.3-codex-spark.
headless_invocation:
Core: `codex exec "<prompt>"` (alias `codex e`). Verified flags on 0.133.0:
- `--json` ŌĆö stream events to stdout as JSONL (one JSON object per line).
- `-o, --output-last-message <FILE>` ŌĆö write the agent's final message to a file (verified: contains just the last agent_message text, e.g. "pong").
- `--output-schema <FILE>` ŌĆö JSON Schema file constraining the model's final response shape (pair with `-o` to get a machine-parseable final JSON).
- Prompt via argv, or stdin: `cat prompt.txt | codex exec -`; if both argv prompt and piped stdin are given, stdin is appended as a `<stdin>` block (useful: `npm test 2>&1 | codex exec "fix failing tests"`).
- `--ephemeral` ŌĆö do not persist session files (use when you never need resume).
- `-C, --cd <DIR>` ŌĆö working root; `--add-dir <DIR>` for extra writable dirs.
- `-i, --image <FILE>...` ŌĆö attach images.
- `--color never` ŌĆö disable ANSI in non-JSON output.
Example, fully non-interactive with structured stream:
`codex exec --json --skip-git-repo-check -s workspace-write -C /path/to/repo -o /tmp/last.txt "implement X" </dev/null`
Verified event stream (exact output): {"type":"thread.started","thread_id":"<uuid>"} ŌåÆ {"type":"turn.started"} ŌåÆ {"type":"item.started"|"item.completed","item":{...}} ŌåÆ {"type":"turn.completed","usage":{...}}. Item types observed: "agent_message" (field: text), "command_execution" (fields: command, aggregated_output, exit_code, status in_progress|completed), "file_change" (changes:[{path,kind:add|...}], status). Docs also list reasoning, mcp_tool_call, web_search, todo_list, error item types. There is also `codex review` / `codex exec review` for non-interactive code review.
status_monitoring:
With `--json`, parse the JSONL stream line-by-line from stdout (events arrive live as the agent works ŌĆö good for streaming progress to a UI):
- Progress: `item.started` / `item.completed` events; `command_execution` items include the command, aggregated_output, and exit_code; `file_change` items list changed paths.
- Completion: `{"type":"turn.completed","usage":{"input_tokens":N,"cached_input_tokens":N,"output_tokens":N,"reasoning_output_tokens":N}}` ŌĆö token usage is right there per turn.
- Errors: `{"type":"error","message":"..."}` followed by `{"type":"turn.failed","error":{"message":"..."}}` (verified with an invalid model: the embedded message contained the raw API 400 JSON).
- Exit codes (verified): 0 on success, 1 on turn failure. So an orchestrator can rely on process exit code for pass/fail and on turn.failed for the error detail.
- The session/thread id for later resume is in the very first event: `thread.started.thread_id`. Capture it.
- Final answer: last `agent_message` item before `turn.completed`, or just read the `-o` file.
- Human-readable progress goes to stderr in non-json mode; with `--json`, stdout is pure JSONL (only stray stderr noise like "Reading additional input from stdin..." ŌĆö keep stderr separate from stdout).
session_management:
`codex exec resume` continues a previous non-interactive session:
- `codex exec resume <SESSION_ID> "follow-up prompt"` ŌĆö SESSION_ID is the UUID from the `thread.started` event (also accepts a thread name). Verified: resuming reused the same thread_id and recalled context from the earlier run across separate processes.
- `codex exec resume --last "prompt"` ŌĆö resume the most recent recorded session; `--all` disables cwd filtering of the session picker (sessions are filtered by cwd by default ŌĆö a gotcha if your orchestrator changes directories).
- Resume supports the same automation flags: `--json`, `-o`, `--output-schema`, `-m`, `-i`, `--skip-git-repo-check`, `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`, `-c` overrides.
- Typical review-loop pattern: run `codex exec --json ... "implement feature"`, capture thread_id, run reviewer, then `codex exec resume <thread_id> --json "Address this review feedback: ..."`.
- Do NOT use `--ephemeral` on the first run if you intend to resume (it skips persisting session files). Sessions live under `~/.codex/sessions` + `session_index.jsonl`. Interactive equivalents: `codex resume --last`, `codex fork` (branch a session).
model_and_effort_selection:
- `-m, --model <MODEL>` on `codex`, `codex exec`, and `codex exec resume`. Current model slugs (from the live models cache on this machine): gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.3-codex-spark (plus codex-auto-review). Local config default here: model = "gpt-5.5".
- Reasoning effort: config key `model_reasoning_effort` with values minimal | low | medium | high | xhigh (per docs; this machine's config.toml uses "xhigh", confirming xhigh is valid). Set per-invocation with a config override: `codex exec -c model_reasoning_effort="low" -m gpt-5.4-mini --json "quick triage task"`.
- `-c, --config <key=value>` overrides any ~/.codex/config.toml key per invocation (dotted paths for nesting; value parsed as TOML). Examples: `-c model="gpt-5.5"`, `-c model_reasoning_effort="high"`.
- `-p, --profile <NAME>` selects a named profile from config.toml; `--profile-v2 <NAME>` layers `$CODEX_HOME/<name>.config.toml` on top of base config ŌĆö handy for defining e.g. "fast" vs "deep" orchestrator presets.
- `--oss` + `--local-provider lmstudio|ollama` for local open-source models.
permissions_automation:
- `-s, --sandbox <MODE>`: read-only | workspace-write | danger-full-access.
- `-a, --ask-for-approval <POLICY>`: untrusted | on-failure (deprecated) | on-request | never. For headless runs use `never` (failures are returned to the model rather than prompting). Note: `codex exec` defaults to non-interactive behavior already; `-a` is on the top-level `codex` command, and config key `approval_policy` can be set via `-c approval_policy="never"` for exec.
- `--full-auto`: still accepted as a hidden/deprecated alias (verified it parses on 0.133.0), but docs say it is replaced by `--sandbox workspace-write`; prefer the explicit flags.
- `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`: no prompts, no sandbox ŌĆö only inside an externally sandboxed environment (CI container, VM).
- `--skip-git-repo-check`: required when cwd is not a git repo (codex exec errors otherwise).
- `-C, --cd <DIR>`: set agent working root without changing your orchestrator's cwd; `--add-dir <DIR>` widens the writable set.
- Hardening/reproducibility for orchestrators: `--ignore-user-config` (skip the user's ~/.codex/config.toml; auth still read from CODEX_HOME), `--ignore-rules` (skip execpolicy .rules), `--strict-config`, `--ephemeral`.
- Recommended CI-ish combo: `codex exec --json -s workspace-write -c approval_policy="never" --skip-git-repo-check -C "$REPO" "<task>" </dev/null`.
remote_capability:
`codex cloud` (marked EXPERIMENTAL) is fully scriptable on 0.133.0:
- Submit: `codex cloud exec --env <ENV_ID> "task prompt"` ŌĆö also `--branch <BRANCH>` (defaults to current) and `--attempts <N>` (best-of-N, default 1). ENV_ID comes from browsing `codex cloud` (TUI) or your Codex cloud environment settings.
- Track: `codex cloud status <TASK_ID>`; `codex cloud list --env <ENV_ID> --limit 20 --cursor <CURSOR> --json` (the `--json` flag on list gives machine-readable task listings; cursor-based pagination, limit 1-20).
- Fetch results: `codex cloud diff <TASK_ID>` (unified diff) and `codex cloud apply <TASK_ID> [--attempt N]` (applies the diff to the local working tree; `--attempt` selects among best-of-N attempts).
- Orchestrator loop: submit ŌåÆ poll `status` ŌåÆ on completion `diff` for review or `apply` to land changes.
Separately, `codex remote-control` ([experimental]) manages an app-server daemon with remote control, and the interactive TUI supports `--remote ws://host:port|wss://|unix://PATH` + `--remote-auth-token-env <ENV_VAR>` to attach to a remote app-server endpoint ŌĆö i.e., you can host the engine remotely and connect clients to it.
gotchas:
1. Stdin: codex exec reads stdin when it's not a TTY ("Reading additional input from stdin..." observed). From Node, spawn with stdin closed (`stdio: ['ignore','pipe','pipe']`) or redirect `</dev/null`, otherwise an open pipe can stall the run or inject garbage as a `<stdin>` block.
2. Git check: exec refuses to run outside a git repo unless `--skip-git-repo-check`.
3. Auth: a ChatGPT login token in `~/.codex/auth.json` (created by interactive `codex login`) is used by child processes automatically; in CI use `CODEX_API_KEY=<key>` env var or pre-seed `$CODEX_HOME/auth.json`. `CODEX_HOME` env var relocates the whole config/auth/session dir ŌĆö set a per-job CODEX_HOME for isolation. Check with `codex login status` / `codex doctor`.
4. config.toml leakage: child processes inherit the user's `~/.codex/config.toml` (model, effort, MCP servers, hooks). For deterministic orchestration pass `--ignore-user-config` plus explicit `-m`/`-c` flags, or a dedicated `--profile-v2`. Note `--ignore-user-config` still uses CODEX_HOME for auth.
5. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 on turn.failed (verified) ŌĆö but a turn can "succeed" while the task is wrong; also parse `turn.failed`/`error` events and validate output (use `--output-schema` for enforced shape).
6. Resume picker filters sessions by cwd by default (`--all` disables) ŌĆö resume by explicit thread_id (captured from `thread.started`) to avoid cwd mismatches. `--ephemeral` runs cannot be resumed.
7. Concurrency: multiple codex exec processes are fine (separate session UUIDs), but they share `~/.codex` sqlite/session state ŌĆö heavy parallelism is safest with per-worker CODEX_HOME dirs; also avoid two agents in `workspace-write` on the same checkout (use git worktrees).
8. stdout vs stderr: with `--json`, treat stdout as pure JSONL and keep stderr separate; in non-json mode progress text interleaves. Lines can be large (aggregated_output of commands is embedded) ŌĆö use a line-based stream parser, not whole-buffer JSON.parse.
9. `--full-auto` is deprecated/hidden; `-a on-failure` is deprecated (prefer `never` for headless). Sandbox `workspace-write` blocks network for shell commands by default (config `sandbox_workspace_write.network_access` to change).
10. For long-lived embedding, prefer `codex app-server` (JSON-RPC over stdio/unix/ws; `codex app-server generate-ts` emits TypeScript protocol bindings, `generate-json-schema` emits schemas; ws auth via capability-token or signed JWT flags) or `codex mcp-server` (Codex as an MCP tool over stdio) over respawning exec per turn. The old `codex proto` interface is gone in this version, replaced by app-server. `codex exec-server` is a further [EXPERIMENTAL] standalone exec service.
11. Token cost: every exec spawn re-sends system/context (~14k input tokens for a trivial prompt observed); resumed turns grow (~30k on second turn). Budget via the `usage` field on each turn.completed.
